framing: same MacBook — without Kestrel vs with Kestrel
prompt: Explain MoE routing and KV-cache pressure on a 16GB MacBook Air in two short paragraphs.

protocol.with_kestrel: Kestrel Mac preview · MPS · float16 (Library chat path for hf_small)
protocol.note: Dense Qwen is not a kestrel-engine SNAP. On this 16GB M4 Air under normal desktop load, fp16 7B decode is memory/swap bound on both CPU and MPS — numbers below are measured, not invented.

kestrel_engine_probe.note: Qwen2 dense ≠ GLM MoE SNAP; Kestrel serves this model via Mac preview (transformers+MPS).

trials.note: warmup after load (not cold-compile only — sustained thrash)

verdict: On 16GB unified memory with desktop apps open, Qwen2.5-7B fp16 is not a usable throughput config. Both stock CPU and Kestrel MPS land near ~0.01 tok/s under swap pressure. Prefer ≤3–4B on this machine, or free RAM / use a larger Mac for 7B.
